question: Did the model output the correct final piece sequence 4, 1, 2, 3 (corresponding to Top-Left, Top-Right, Bottom-Left, Bottom-Right)?
Does the model correctly identify that Image 1 is rotated upside down (180 degrees) and requires rotation to be upright?
Does the model correctly identify that Image 4 is rotated sideways and requires a 90-degree rotation to make the trees and signpost upright?
Does the model identify the vertical connection between Image 4 (Top-Left) and Image 2 (Bottom-Left) based on the continuity of the man in the purple jacket or the white-haired person?
Does the model identify the vertical connection between Image 1 (Top-Right) and Image 3 (Bottom-Right) based on the continuity of the bass guitar (neck in Image 1, body in Image 3)?
Does the model identify the horizontal connection between Image 2 (Bottom-Left) and Image 3 (Bottom-Right) based on the continuity of the acoustic guitar, rug, or foreground equipment?
Does the model identify the horizontal connection between Image 4 (Top-Left) and Image 1 (Top-Right) based on the continuity of the sky, clouds, or tree line?
In the generated images, are Image 1 and Image 4 actually displayed in the correct upright orientation (e.g., trees growing upward, text readable), correcting the original rotations?
If the model generates an image combining Image 4 and Image 2 vertically, are the lines of the man's purple jacket aligned continuously without significant offset or cut-off parts?
If the model generates an image combining Image 1 and Image 3 vertically, does the neck of the bass guitar in Image 1 align correctly with the body of the bass guitar in Image 3?
Do the generated intermediate or final images preserve the legibility of text elements (e.g., "HARMONY RETREAT", "STUDIO B", "LAKE 2KM") without mirroring or corruption?
Are the seams between adjacent pieces in the generated images visually smooth, avoiding obvious gaps, overlaps, or sharp color discontinuities (e.g., in the sky gradient)?
In the final reconstructed image (if generated), does the visual arrangement match the sequence 4(TL), 1(TR), 2(BL), 3(BR) with all pieces correctly oriented and positioned?
If multiple intermediate images are generated, do the pieces remain in consistent relative positions and orientations across steps (no swapping or drifting)?
If the model describes rotating a specific image (e.g., Image 1 or 4), does the corresponding generated image visually reflect that rotation?
If the model describes placing a piece in a specific relative position (e.g., "Image 4 to the left of Image 1"), does the generated image visually show that exact spatial arrangement?
Does the model correctly interpret and describe the visual content of the pieces (e.g., identifying instruments, signs, or clothing colors) in its text reasoning?
Are there any contradictions between the textual description of piece positions/orientations and the visual evidence shown in the generated images?
